revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa), kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|5.94|N|135|52|23.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! Type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | Nishizuka Kofun || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa), kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|5.94|N|135|52|23.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa), kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|5.94|N|135|52|23.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa), kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|5.94|N|135|52|23.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa), kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|5.94|N|135|52|23.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[cinnabar]]. [[Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Kaminaka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18337677]] Wikidata name: Nishizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337677]] Wikidata name: Nishizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337677]] Wikidata name: Nishizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)}} [[ja:西塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q18337677}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337677]] Wikidata name: Nishizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]]. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi]] and [[:en:Owari Province|Owari regions]], indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]]. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kaminaka Station|Kaminaka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}} railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field}}. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi}} and {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari regions}}, indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with {{ill|Cinnabar|en|Cinnabar|WD=Q104614|lt=Cinnabar}}. {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}}. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|WD=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | Nishizuka Kofun || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}} railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field}}. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi}} and {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari regions}}, indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with {{ill|Cinnabar|en|Cinnabar|WD=Q104614|lt=Cinnabar}}. {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}}. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|WD=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | Nishizuka Kofun || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-nishizuka.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nishizuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nishizuka Kofun]][[ja:西塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nishizuka Kofun | native_name = 西塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nishizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468611|N|135.873028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nishizuka Kofun'''|西塚古墳|Nishizuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201586 |title= 西塚古墳 |trans-title= Nishzuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishizuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is located west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway and to the west of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 74 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 39 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 47 meters. However, most of the rectangular portion of the structure was destroyed during the construction of the Obama Line railroad in August 1916, and only the posterior circular portion of tumulus survives isolated in the middle of a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field|1=Paddy field}}. The round portion has a height of 6.7 meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the surrounding fields. The ''haniwa'' was recognized to have the characteristics of the {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi|1=Kingdom of Kibi}} and {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari regions|1=Owari Province}}, indicating some relationship with those regions. The tumulus was also originally surrounded by a 20-meter wide moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the railroad construction in 1918, the 5.46 x 1.3 meter stone vertical-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered and numerous [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] were excavated. The burial chamber had a height of 1.5 meters and was painted red with {{ill|Cinnabar|en|Cinnabar|WD=Q104614|lt=Cinnabar|1=Cinnabar}}. {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, possibly of Chinese origin, ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, gold earrings, metal belt fittings, horse ornaments, and weapons including swords, spears and fragments of armor were found, all of which are currently in the possession of the [[Museum of the Imperial Collections]] in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Judging from these artifacts, the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century AD (the middle [[Kofun period]]), and it has been speculated that this is the tomb of Kashiwade-no-Omi Ikaruga, which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site was subsequently [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and in 1991, and in 2008 by {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}}. The tumulus is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|WD=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
